news.title: Trump Accounts for kids launch July 4: What parents need to know
news.summary: Trump accounts are free, but there are restrictions on how and when they can be withdrawn. Here's a breakdown of how Trump Accounts work, who qualifies and when.

news.title: Palantir's Karp bashes OpenAI, Anthropic token model: 'Something has gone completely wrong'
news.summary: Karp says token costs are forcing companies to choose open weight models. He says companies are choosing to prioritize efficiency over so-called tokenmaxxing.

news.title: Meta pops 10% as company makes cloud push to sell excess AI compute power capacity
news.summary: Some investors have been uneasy about the company's infrastructure spending plans. The new business is a welcome signal for some investors who have been wary of the company.

news.title: Trump says outside funds 'run my money' after disclosure shows billions in 2025 revenue
news.summary: The 927-page annual financial disclosure form shows Trump's purchases and sales of hundreds of companies' stocks. The list includes Nvidia, Amazon and Microsoft.

news.title: GM reports 4.2% decline in Q2 U.S. sales as EV demand falls
news.summary: General Motors' second-quarter U.S. sales fell 4.2% as year-over-year demand for its all-electric vehicles and Chevrolet Silverado pickup trucks declined.

news.title: ‘I don’t think I’ll make it to 80’: I’m 70 and single. Do I take out a reverse mortgage or a home-equity agreement?
news.summary: “I’m torn between these two options,” he says. “I'm torn between this and that.”

news.title: Opening a ‘Trump account’ for your children? Here is the risk you need to recognize first.
news.summary: The new accounts ban bonds and international stocks, forcing parents to bet their children’s financial future entirely on U.S. equities.

news.title: This is the best time ‘in a generation’ to buy space and defense stocks, analysts say
news.summary: Wedbush analysts are taking a bullish view of the  space sector. They are initiating coverage of SpaceX and other stocks. Wedbush: We have a 'buy' rating on SpaceX.

news.title: Warsh tells Wall Street to stop looking to the Fed for clues on interest rates — and start watching the economic data
news.summary: New Federal Reserve chief Kevin Warsh gave no hint as to whether the central bank would raise interest rates in July. Warsh repeated his dislike of so-called forward guidance to alert investors to future Fed actions.

news.title: Here’s what’s worth streaming in July 2026 on Netflix, Hulu, HBO Max and more
news.summary: This month is a good month to save on streaming, despite the return of ‘Enola Holmes’ to Netflix and ‘Silo” to Apple.
